channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17404] Support RHEL 9.4
item.description: Please port lustre to RHEL 9.4 once available.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55487/
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: update RHEL 9.4 [5.14.0-427.22.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 72cc77e642d6cb696532f3c902680ac04340aa08
Support patches have landed for Lustre 2.16.0 and 2.15.5 releases.
An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4: 5.14.0-427.24.1.el9_4: LU-18018

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55685
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: update RHEL 9.4 [5.14.0-427.24.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 67a54dc3921501ca79e9a0d30c87c0327443cc2b

Subject: LU-17404 kernel: RHEL 9.4 server support
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 94bd78a44f7bfb5fcc9e272eab9ee3f06963b531

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55454/
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: fix filemap_splice_read detection
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: befb4e43590fff7f3b0d42e5928e91a76dc223f0

An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4: 5.14.0-427.24.1.el9_4
https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2024:4349?sc_cid=701600000006NHXAA2

Security Fix(es):

  • kernel: net/mlx5e: Fix operation precedence bug in port timestamping napi_poll context (CVE-2023-52626)
  • kernel: Bluetooth: Avoid potential use-after-free in hci_error_reset (CVE-2024-26801)
  • kernel: crypto: qat - resolve race condition during AER recovery (CVE-2024-26974)
  • kernel: xen-netfront: Add missing skb_mark_for_recycle (CVE-2024-27393)
  • kernel: net/mlx5e: fix a potential double-free in fs_any_create_groups (CVE-2023-52667)
  • kernel: smb: client: fix UAF in smb2_reconnect_server() (CVE-2024-35870)
  • kernel: net/mlx5: Properly link new fs rules into the tree (CVE-2024-35960)
  • kernel: net: hns3: do not allow call hns3_nic_net_open repeatedly (CVE-2021-47400)

Bug Fix(es):

  • cifs - kernel panic with cifs_put_smb_ses (JIRA:RHEL-28943)
  • BUG: unable to handle page fault for address: ff16bf752f593ff8 [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-35672)
  • [HPE 9.4 Bug] Request merge of AMD address translation library patch series [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-36220)
  • [RHEL9] kernel BUG at lib/list_debug.c:51! [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-36687)
  • ice: DPLL-related fixes [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-36716)
  • CNB95: net/sched: update TC core to upstream v6.8 [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-37641)
  • IPv6: SR: backport fixes from upstream [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-37669)
  • [RFE] Backport tmpfs noswap mount option [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-38252)
  • Isolated cores causing issues on latest RHEL9.4 kernel and not functioning as desired. [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-38595)
  • [ice] Add automatic VF reset on Tx MDD events [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-39083)
  • [HPEMC RHEL 9.4 REGRESSION] turbostat: turbostat broken on 10+ sockets. [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-34953)
  • bnx2x: fix crashes in PCI error handling, resource leaks [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-43272)

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54712/
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: new kernel [RHEL 9.4 5.14.0-427.20.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 26ef616817d7f158fb2313ca59a2d8737669313b

Subject: LU-17404 kernel: update RHEL 9.4 [5.14.0-427.22.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 6c116ac5e0bc0fa029021ba92fa99ad4911757a9

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55487
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: update RHEL 9.4 [5.14.0-427.22.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: c0380c9cdf46b0493d2c9e28cd29439ba8833193

An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4: 5.14.0-427.22.1.el9_4
https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHBA-2024:3983?sc_cid=701600000006NHXAA2

Bug Fix(es):

  • [DELL 9.4 BUG] - Screen shows garbage sometimes [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-36534)
  • cifs - Unable to mount CIFS shares after upgrading to RHEL9.4 (JIRA:RHEL-36743)
  • [Azure][RHEL-9][MANA]System crash at skbuff when set mtu=100 [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-37622)
  • selective backport from perf upstream v6.8 to fix AmpereOne and Nvidia support [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-34991)
  • [Intel 9.5 FEAT] [SRF] performance: hwmon: (coretemp) Fix core count limitation [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-35447)
  • CNB95: dpll: rebase DPLL to upstream v6.8 [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-36572)
  • ice: IRQs being moved to isolated CPUs and causing latency issues [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-38512)
  • Bluetooth device disappears from the device list after a few minutes with AX211 [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-38524)
  • deadlock on dfs_cache_refresh [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-38904)
  • [DELL 9.3 BUG]Unexpected message "integrity: Problem loading X.509 certificate -126" occurred on boot screen. [rhel-9.4.z] (JIRA:RHEL-39933)
  • [rhel9-rt-debug] BUG: sleeping function called from invalid context at kernel/locking/spinlock_rt.c:46 ( clocksource_mark_unstable) (JIRA:RHEL-9296)

"Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55454
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: fix filemap_splice_read detection
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a212002d827b06b7f61b0d48c4cd656ede090c60

Commit: 527a21ce444b46034e45de185a3bd39727353abb

An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4: 5.14.0-427.20.1.el9_4
https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2024:3619?sc_cid=701600000006NHXAA2

Security Fix(es):

  • kernel: ipv6: sr: fix possible use-after-free and null-ptr-deref (CVE-2024-26735)
  • kernel: fs: sysfs: Fix reference leak in sysfs_break_active_protection() (CVE-2024-26993)

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55204/
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: update RHEL 9.4 [5.14.0-427.18.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 0cceeec27949ef696f21ebe28ed804884bd4a335

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54717/
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: new kernel [RHEL 9.4 5.14.0-427.16.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 87ccaec2734a0f213252d94b00cca2f0c03d0bdc

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55204
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: update RHEL 9.4 [5.14.0-427.18.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 5aad8ff6a93107c000c0b61d96c9313d3b1e3325

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55203
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: update RHEL 9.4 [5.14.0-427.18.1.el9_4]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 4852d9e2cc31b905e8d5fd09b8658458fd3174ea

An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4: 5.14.0-427.18.1.el9_4
https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2024:3306?sc_cid=701600000006NHXAA2
Security Fix(es):

  • kernel: netfilter: nf_tables: mark set as dead when unbinding anonymous set with timeout (CVE-2024-26643)
  • kernel: netfilter: nf_tables: disallow anonymous set with timeout flag (CVE-2024-26642)
  • kernel: netfilter: nft_ct: sanitize layer 3 and 4 protocol number in custom expectations (CVE-2024-26673)
  • kernel: net: ip_tunnel: prevent perpetual headroom growth (CVE-2024-26804)

An update for kernel is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4: 5.14.0-427.16.1.el9_4
https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2024:2758?sc_cid=701600000006NHXAA2

Security Fix(es):

  • kernel: Marvin vulnerability side-channel leakage in the RSA decryption operation (CVE-2023-6240)
  • CVE-2024-25743 hw: amd: Instruction raise #VC exception at exit (AMD-SN-3008,CVE-2024-25742,CVE-2024-25743)

Bug Fix(es):

  • ffdhe* algortihms introduced in 0a2e5b909023 as .fips_allowed=1 lack pairwise consistency tests (JIRA:RHEL-27009)
  • mm/mglru: fix underprotected page cache (JIRA:RHEL-29235)
  • [EMR] [TBOOT OS] SUT could not go to S3 state with RHEL 9.2 Tboot OS One CPU return -16 running BUSY (JIRA:RHEL-29673)
  • system hangs completely - NMI not possible (JIRA:RHEL-30678)
  • ice 0000:6f:00.0: PTP failed to get time (JIRA:RHEL-30110)

After applying patch series https://review.whamcloud.com/54713 ("LU-17404 kernel: RHEL 9.4 server support") to master branch, RHEL 9.4 client and server build passed.

runtests also passed: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/0a354593-49a5-49ec-a9ae-6c6c854a9758

There are conflicts while applying the ldiskfs patch series

Just resolved all of the conflicts.
Now updating the patch series https://review.whamcloud.com/54713 ("LU-17404 kernel: RHEL 9.4 server support") based on patch series https://review.whamcloud.com/52919 ("LU-16350 ldiskfs: Server support for LTS linux v6.6").

Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4 - Now Generally Available!
https://access.redhat.com/announcements/7068084
Kernel version: 5.14.0-427.13.1.el9_4

An update for kernel version is now available: 5.14.0-427.13.1.el9_4
https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2024:2394?sc_cid=701600000006NHXAA2

Security Fix(es):

  • kernel: GSM multiplexing race condition leads to privilege escalation (CVE-2023-6546)
  • kernel: multiple use-after-free vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-1086, CVE-2023-3567, CVE-2023-4133, CVE-2023-6932, CVE-2023-39198, CVE-2023-51043, CVE-2023-51779, CVE-2023-51780, CVE-2024-1085, CVE-2024-26582)
  • kernel: Bluetooth BR/EDR PIN Pairing procedure is vulnerable to an impersonation attack (CVE-2020-26555)
  • kernel: memcg does not limit the number of POSIX file locks allowing memory exhaustion (CVE-2022-0480)
  • kernel: multiple NULL pointer dereference vulnerabilities (CVE-2022-38096, CVE-2023-6622, CVE-2023-6915, CVE-2023-42754, CVE-2023-46862, CVE-2023-52574, CVE-2024-0841, CVE-2023-52448)
  • kernel: integer overflow in l2cap_config_req() in net/bluetooth/l2cap_core.c (CVE-2022-45934)
  • kernel: netfilter: nf_tables: out-of-bounds access in nf_tables_newtable() (CVE-2023-6040)
  • kernel: GC's deletion of an SKB races with unix_stream_read_generic() leading to UAF (CVE-2023-6531)
  • kernel: Out of boundary write in perf_read_group() as result of overflow a perf_event's read_size (CVE-2023-6931)
  • kernel: Bluetooth Forward and Future Secrecy Attacks and Defenses (CVE-2023-24023)
  • kernel: irdma: Improper access control (CVE-2023-25775)
  • Kernel: double free in hci_conn_cleanup of the bluetooth subsystem (CVE-2023-28464)
  • kernel: Bluetooth: HCI: global out-of-bounds access in net/bluetooth/hci_sync.c (CVE-2023-28866)
  • kernel: race condition between HCIUARTSETPROTO and HCIUARTGETPROTO in hci_uart_tty_ioctl (CVE-2023-31083)
  • kernel: multiple out-of-bounds read vulnerabilities (CVE-2023-37453, CVE-2023-39189, CVE-2023-39193, CVE-2023-6121, CVE-2023-39194)
  • kernel: netfilter: race condition between IPSET_CMD_ADD and IPSET_CMD_SWAP (CVE-2023-42756)
  • kernel: lib/kobject.c vulnerable to fill_kobj_path out-of-bounds write (CVE-2023-45863)
  • kernel: smb: client: fix potential OOBs in smb2_parse_contexts() (CVE-2023-52434)
  • kernel: mm/sparsemem: fix race in accessing memory_section->usage (CVE-2023-52489)
  • kernel: net: fix possible store tearing in neigh_periodic_work() (CVE-2023-52522)
  • kernel: multiple memory leak vulnerabilities (CVE-2023-52529, CVE-2023-52581)
  • kernel: net: bridge: data races indata-races in br_handle_frame_finish() (CVE-2023-52578)
  • kernel: net/core: kernel crash in ETH_P_1588 flow dissector (CVE-2023-52580)
  • kernel: net/sched: act_ct: fix skb leak and crash on ooo frags (CVE-2023-52610)
  • kernel: CIFS Filesystem Decryption Improper Input Validation Remote Code Execution Vulnerability in function receive_encrypted_standard of client (CVE-2024-0565)
  • kernel: tls: race between async notify and socket close (CVE-2024-26583)
  • kernel: tls: handle backlogging of crypto requests (CVE-2024-26584)
  • kernel: tls: race between tx work scheduling and socket close (CVE-2024-26585)
  • kernel: mlxsw: spectrum_acl_tcam: Fix stack corruption (CVE-2024-26586)
  • kernel: i2c: i801: Fix block process call transactions (CVE-2024-26593)
  • kernel: sched/membarrier: reduce the ability to hammer on sys_membarrier (CVE-2024-26602)
  • kernel: netfilter: nf_tables: reject QUEUE/DROP verdict parameters (CVE-2024-26609)
  • kernel: local dos vulnerability in scatterwalk_copychunks (CVE-2023-6176)
  • kernel: perf/x86/lbr: Filter vsyscall addresses (CVE-2023-52476)
  • kernel: netfilter: nf_tables: disallow timeout for anonymous sets (CVE-2023-52620)
  • kernel: ip6_tunnel: fix NEXTHDR_FRAGMENT handling in ip6_tnl_parse_tlv_enc_lim() (CVE-2024-26633)
Thank you nscfreny. I'll add it to the b2_15 patch series.

b2_15 zfs server build successful (zfs-2.1.15) with:
https://review.whamcloud.com/54717
+ cherry-pick:

2de1dbd440 LU-16350 osd-ldiskfs: no_llseek removed, dquot_transfer
Thanks, b2_15 client build successful on RHEL 9.4 beta.

Yes, I suspect LU-16962 is also needed?

Hi nscfreny, I only incorporated the fix from https://review.whamcloud.com/51670 ("LU-16962 build: parallel configure cleanup") into https://review.whamcloud.com/54700 ("LU-16351 llite: Linux 6.1 prandom, folios_contig, vma_iterator").
Now, the patch series for Lustre b2_15 client support on RHEL 9.4 is https://review.whamcloud.com/54717.

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54717
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: new kernel [RHEL 9.4 5.14.0-427.el9]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ec1894b5ade820aaac62901eb13236b0c78feb02

Subject: LU-17404 kernel: RHEL 9.4 server support

There are conflicts while applying the ldiskfs patch series:

rhel9.2/ext4-prealloc.patch
3 out of 11 hunks FAILED -- saving rejects to file fs/ext4/mballoc.c.rej

linux-5.18/ext4-mballoc-pa-free-mismatch.patch
1 out of 1 hunk FAILED -- saving rejects to file fs/ext4/mballoc.h.rej

rhel9.3/ext4-data-in-dirent.patch
1 out of 32 hunks FAILED -- saving rejects to file fs/ext4/namei.c.rej

rhel9.2/ext4-pdirop.patch
1 out of 1 hunk FAILED -- saving rejects to file fs/ext4/super.c.rej

linux-5.16/ext4-corrupted-inode-block-bitmaps-handling-patches.patch
1 out of 3 hunks FAILED -- saving rejects to file fs/ext4/balloc.c.rej

linux-5.10/ext4-attach-jinode-in-writepages.patch
1 out of 3 hunks FAILED -- saving rejects to file fs/ext4/inode.c.rej

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54713
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: RHEL 9.4 server support
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: c4f2b1636b0dbb880cdf5dbf1553b1bdaa8e06bf

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54712
Subject: LU-17404 kernel: new kernel [RHEL 9.4 5.14.0-427.el9]
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: f2b3f6c924b3afa21b4fd1640db5d50f178c15c9
Yes, I suspect LU-16962 is also needed?
Yes, Fredrik. More patches are needed. I'll make a patch series.

I did try that, got sort of same error but on line 407 of lustre/llite/vvp_dev.c
Seems like HAVE_FILEMAP_GET_FOLIOS_CONTIG was not defined?

Also tried to build zfs server and got:

  CC [M]  /home/freny/git/lustre-release/lustre/ofd/ofd_access_log.o
/home/freny/git/lustre-release/lustre/ofd/ofd_access_log.c:436:19: error: lvalue required as unary ‘&’ operand
  436 |         .llseek = &no_llseek,
      |                   ^

Perhaps LU-16350 is also needed for server?

Hi nscfreny,

b2_15 client build failed, find_get_pages_contig() removed since kernel-5.14.0-380.el9.

Could you please try https://review.whamcloud.com/54700 for Lustre b2_15 branch?

b2_15 client build failed, find_get_pages_contig() removed in kernel-5.14.0-380.el9.

I'm back-porting https://review.whamcloud.com/49232 ("LU-16351 llite: Linux 6.1 prandom, folios_contig, vma_iterator") to Lustre b2_15 branch.

Btw, can't access git://git.whamcloud.com/fs/lustre-release.git today?

Same here.

b2_15 client build failed, find_get_pages_contig() removed since kernel-5.14.0-380.el9.

  CC [M]  /home/freny/git/lustre-release/lustre/llite/vvp_dev.o
/home/freny/git/lustre-release/lustre/llite/vvp_dev.c: In function ‘vvp_pgcache_current’:
/home/freny/git/lustre-release/lustre/llite/vvp_dev.c:419:22: error: implicit declaration of function ‘find_get_pages_contig’; did you mean ‘find_get_pages_tag’? [-Werror=implicit-function-declaration]
  419 |                 nr = find_get_pages_contig(inode->i_mapping,
      |                      ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                      find_get_pages_tag
cc1: all warnings being treated as errors

C9S commit: becfeb26eca4 filemap: remove find_get_pages_contig()
Upstream: https://lkml.kernel.org/r/20220824004023.77310-8-vishal.moola@gmail.com

master (2.15.61_225_gbb6a2d2) client build ok.

Btw, can't access git://git.whamcloud.com/fs/lustre-release.git today?

Release Notes for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.4 Beta:
https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9-beta/html/9.4_release_notes/index
